proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively associated with pfs_months as a continuous prognostic factor.
feature_005 (3-level: 0/1/2) is monotonically associated with pfs_months, with higher levels associated with shorter PFS (consistent with a performance-status-like prognostic variable).
feature_006 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006 = 0.
feature_013 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_013 = 0.
feature_001 = 1 (rare, ~10% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_001 = 0.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.spearmanr(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman r = +0.7264, p < 1e-300 across n=50000. Very strong positive association - feature_012 is the strongest single continuous prognostic factor.
Mean PFS: feature_005=0 -> 5.640 (n=17592); =1 -> 4.444 (n=24971); =2 -> 3.289 (n=7437). Strict monotonic decrease, ANOVA F=2860.75, p < 1e-300. Per-level decrement ~ -1.18 months.
Mean PFS: feature_006=0 -> 5.153 (n=35031); =1 -> 3.617 (n=14969). Difference -1.536 months, ANOVA p < 1e-300.
Mean PFS: feature_013=0 -> 4.310 (n=32572); =1 -> 5.408 (n=17428). Difference +1.098 months in favor of feature_013=1, ANOVA p < 1e-300.
Mean PFS: feature_001=0 -> 4.800 (n=44910); =1 -> 3.750 (n=5090). Difference -1.050 months, ANOVA p ~ 1.8e-179.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of feature_028 = 1, feature_029 = 1, feature_023 = 1, feature_018 = 1 is associated with a difference in pfs_months of at least 0.3 months in either direction relative to its complement.
feature_024 (continuous, ~albumin-like 1.5-5.5 range) is positively correlated with pfs_months; feature_033 (continuous, range 1-100) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_004 (continuous, range 0-25) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.spearmanr(df['feature_024'], df['pfs_months']); stats.spearmanr(df['feature_033'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_024: Spearman r=+0.1019 (p~1.4e-115); feature_033: Spearman r=-0.0828 (p~8.1e-77). Both significant, feature_024 favorable / feature_033 unfavorable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model regressing pfs_months on all 37 features, feature_012, feature_005, feature_006, feature_013, feature_001, feature_028, feature_029, feature_018, feature_024, feature_033, and feature_004 each remain independent prognostic factors for pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.792, n=50000. Independent significant coefficients: feature_012 +0.176/unit, feature_005 -1.172/level, feature_006 -1.549, feature_013 +1.101, feature_001 -0.983, feature_029 +0.532, feature_028 -0.582, feature_018 -0.471, feature_024 +0.476/unit, feature_033 -0.019/unit, feature_004 -0.077/unit, feature_023 not independently significant (p=0.32). Strong simultaneous independence supports the marginal-prognosis findings.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features that show large main effects on pfs_months, feature_013 is the most plausible treatment variable: a screen of pairwise binary x binary interactions on pfs_months will identify multiple feature_013-by-other-feature interactions with very small p-values, indicating that feature_013's effect is concentrated in subgroups defined by other binary features.

analyses.code:
for a,b in combinations(binary_cols,2):
    m = smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {a}*{b}', df).fit(); print(a,b,m.params[f'{a}:{b}'], m.pvalues[f'{a}:{b}'])
analyses.result_summary: Top pairwise interactions all involve feature_013: feature_013:feature_028 beta=-1.62 p=4e-255; feature_013:feature_029 beta=+1.59 p=9e-229; feature_013:feature_018 beta=-1.36 p=2e-118; feature_013:feature_023 beta=+1.09 p=3e-117; feature_013:feature_016 beta=+0.42 p=9e-19. No competing 'treatment-like' binary feature shows comparable interaction signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_013 = 1 on pfs_months is positive when feature_029 = 1 and negligible when feature_029 = 0 (feature_029 is a predictive biomarker for feature_013).
The effect of feature_013 = 1 on pfs_months is positive when feature_028 = 0 and negligible when feature_028 = 1 (feature_028 = 1 abolishes the feature_013 benefit).
The effect of feature_013 = 1 on pfs_months is positive when feature_018 = 0 and negligible when feature_018 = 1 (feature_018 = 1 abolishes the feature_013 benefit).

analyses.result_summary: Cell means by feature_029 x feature_013: feature_029=0,feature_013=0 -> 4.303; feature_029=0,feature_013=1 -> 4.285 (TE=-0.02); feature_029=1,feature_013=0 -> 4.313; feature_029=1,feature_013=1 -> 5.890 (TE=+1.58). feature_029=1 enables the treatment effect.

Cell means: feature_028=0,feature_013=0 -> 4.307; feature_028=0,feature_013=1 -> 5.966 (TE=+1.66); feature_028=1,feature_013=0 -> 4.316; feature_028=1,feature_013=1 -> 4.352 (TE=+0.04). feature_028=1 abolishes the feature_013 benefit.

Cell means: feature_018=0,feature_013=0 -> 4.307; feature_018=0,feature_013=1 -> 5.657 (TE=+1.35); feature_018=1,feature_013=0 -> 4.322; feature_018=1,feature_013=1 -> 4.308 (TE=-0.01). feature_018=1 abolishes the feature_013 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint subgroup feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0, the effect of feature_013=1 on pfs_months is substantially larger than +1.1 month (the unconditional main effect).
Outside that joint subgroup, the effect of feature_013=1 on pfs_months is approximately zero.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 (n=18681), feature_013 coefficient = +2.908 months (p < 1e-300). Treatment effect ~2.6x the marginal effect.
Outside the joint biomarker subgroup (n=31319), feature_013 coefficient = +0.003 months (p=0.91). Treatment effect is statistically null outside the subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the binary biomarker-positive subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0), the size of the feature_013 treatment effect is further modulated by the continuous variable feature_033, with the treatment effect declining as feature_033 increases.

analyses.code:
sub = df[(df.feature_029==1) & (df.feature_028==0) & (df.feature_018==0)].copy()
sub['f033c'] = sub.feature_033 - sub.feature_033.mean()
smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * f033c', sub).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker+ subgroup (n=18681): feature_013 coef +2.916 (p<1e-300), feature_033 main effect -0.0005 (p=0.79, not significant), interaction feature_013:feature_033 = -0.135 (p<1e-300). For each 1-unit increase in feature_033, feature_013 effect decreases by 0.135 months. feature_033 is a strong PREDICTIVE (not prognostic) modifier within biomarker+ subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists a sharp threshold of feature_033 around 15 such that within the biomarker+ subgroup, treatment effect is large (>+4 months) when feature_033 <= 15 and approximately zero when feature_033 > 15.

analyses.code:
sub = df[(df.feature_029==1) & (df.feature_028==0) & (df.feature_018==0)]
for th in [5,7.5,10,12.5,15,17.5,20,25]:
    s_lo = sub[sub.feature_033<=th]; s_hi = sub[sub.feature_033>th]
    print(th, te_lo, te_hi)
analyses.result_summary: Threshold scan within biomarker+ subgroup: th=10 TE_lo=+4.96 vs TE_hi=+1.57; th=12.5 TE_lo=+4.94 vs TE_hi=+0.71; th=15 TE_lo=+4.65 vs TE_hi=+0.02; th=17.5 TE_lo=+4.16 vs TE_hi=-0.05; th=20 TE_lo=+3.83 vs TE_hi=-0.03. Threshold around 15 cleanly separates responders (TE~+4.65) from non-responders (TE~0); ~5 unit window where benefit drops from +5 to 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete predictive biomarker subgroup for the feature_013 treatment effect is feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033<=15. Within this subgroup the treatment effect on pfs_months exceeds +4 months; outside, the treatment effect is statistically zero.

analyses.result_summary: Within full biomarker+ subgroup (n=11575): feature_013 coef +4.651 (p<1e-300, treated mean 8.94 vs untreated 4.29). Outside (n=38425): coef +0.006 (p=0.78). Joint model feature_013 * biomarker_pos: feature_013 main = +0.006 (p=0.79), biomarker_pos main = -0.032 (p=0.26), interaction = +4.644 (p<1e-300). Almost ALL the treatment benefit is concentrated in this 23%-of-cohort subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-positive subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 & feature_033<=15), removing any one of the four predicates substantially weakens the treatment effect (each component is required, not redundant).

analyses.result_summary: Drop feature_029=1 -> include feature_029=0 with f033<=15: TE in those is +0.04 vs +2.50 in feature_029=1; drop feature_028=0 -> in feature_028=1 with f033<=15 TE=+0.02 vs +2.71 in feature_028=0; drop feature_018=0 -> in feature_018=1 with f033<=15 TE=+0.03 vs +2.17; drop feature_033<=15 -> within 3-binary biomarker+, with f033>15 TE=+0.02 vs +4.65 with f033<=15. Each of the four predicates is independently necessary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features that showed marginal interactions with feature_013 (feature_023, feature_016) do NOT further refine the responder subgroup once feature_029, feature_028, feature_018, and feature_033 are already conditioned on.

analyses.result_summary: Within full 4-marker biomarker+ subgroup: feature_023=0 TE=+4.33 (n=1711) vs feature_023=1 TE=+4.71 (n=9864); feature_016=0 TE=+4.58 (n=6346) vs feature_016=1 TE=+4.74 (n=5229). Differences <0.4 months and not consistent enough to add as required predicates - feature_023 and feature_016 show small modulation but the four-marker definition captures essentially all the heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features (feature_011, feature_030, feature_019, feature_015, feature_022, feature_032, feature_008, feature_009, feature_026, feature_020) are NOT additional treatments: a comprehensive interaction screen using each of them as the index variable will not produce treatment-by-feature interaction p-values comparable to those for feature_013.

analyses.code: for tx in candidate_set: for x in features: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {tx}*{x}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction screens for each candidate: smallest p-value across all (candidate x feature) pairs other than feature_013-anchored ones is feature_019:feature_013 p=4.3e-3 (which is feature_019 acting on the feature_013 treatment, not feature_019 itself being a treatment). No alternative treatment candidate produces interaction p < 1e-3 with any other feature. feature_013 is the only treatment-like variable in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (3-level performance-status-like variable) is purely PROGNOSTIC for pfs_months, not predictive of feature_013 treatment effect: the feature_013 x feature_005 interaction has p > 0.5.
feature_006 (binary, ~30% prevalence, big main effect on PFS) is purely PROGNOSTIC, not predictive of feature_013 treatment effect.
feature_001 (binary, ~10% prevalence, unfavorable) is purely PROGNOSTIC, not predictive of feature_013 treatment effect.
feature_012 (continuous, age-like) is mostly prognostic; the interaction with feature_013 is at most very small in magnitude.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013:C(feature_005)[T.1] = -0.021 (p=0.66); feature_013:C(feature_005)[T.2] = +0.035 (p=0.60). No predictive signal for feature_005 - it is purely prognostic.
feature_013:feature_006 = +0.006 (p=0.90). Purely prognostic.
feature_013:feature_001 = -0.041 (p=0.59). Purely prognostic.

feature_013:feature_012 = -0.003 per unit (p=0.044, marginal). Across the full age-like range of 60 units that is at most a 0.18 month modulation - clinically negligible compared to the >4 month effect of the biomarker subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-positive subgroup, no continuous variable other than feature_033 modulates the feature_013 treatment effect at p < 0.01.

analyses.code: for c in continuous_cols (except f033): smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ feature_013 * {c}_centered', sub_pos).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 (n=18681), only feature_033 produces an interaction with feature_013 at p < 0.01 (p<1e-300). Other continuous variables (feature_012, feature_036, feature_024, feature_031, feature_004, feature_021, feature_003, feature_025, feature_027, feature_034, feature_014, feature_037, feature_017, feature_035, feature_002, feature_007, feature_010): all p > 0.01. feature_033 is the unique continuous modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_033 is independent of treatment assignment feature_013 (correlation ~ 0), confirming feature_033 is a true effect modifier (predictive biomarker), not a confounder.
feature_029, feature_028, feature_018 are each independent of feature_013 assignment (correlations near 0), consistent with random or random-like assignment of feature_013 in this cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.0052. Not statistically associated; treatment is balanced with respect to this biomarker.
feature_029 r=+0.003; feature_028 r=-0.003; feature_018 r=+0.006. All near zero; treatment assignment is balanced across these biomarkers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 & feature_033<=15), the prognostic effects of feature_005, feature_006, feature_001, and feature_012 on pfs_months persist with magnitudes similar to those in the full cohort (so prognostic features carry through but do not modify the treatment effect).

analyses.result_summary: Within full biomarker+ subgroup, feature_005 effect estimates: T.1 -1.155, T.2 -2.432; feature_006 main -1.5 magnitude (similar to full cohort). Interactions of feature_005 and feature_006 with feature_013 remain non-significant (p>0.3). Prognostic features act additively on top of treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Searching all triples of binary features for the subgroup with the largest treatment effect of feature_013 yields combinations dominated by {feature_029=1, feature_028=0, feature_018=0} and rotations - confirming the predictive biomarker structure.

analyses.code: for combo in combinations(top_modifiers,3): for vals in product([0,1],3): compute TE
analyses.result_summary: Top triples (n>500 in both arms): (feature_029=1, feature_028=0, feature_016=1) TE=+2.92 (n=8518); (feature_029=1, feature_028=0, feature_018=0) TE=+2.91 (n=18681); (feature_028=0, feature_018=0, feature_023=1) TE=+2.75 (n=17153); (feature_028=0, feature_023=1, feature_016=1) TE=+2.73 (n=7801). All best subgroups share feature_028=0 and either feature_029=1 or feature_018=0 - converging on the same biomarker structure. Largest negative subgroup TE only -0.19 (no anti-predictive subgroup).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stronger treatment effect in feature_023=1 (vs 0) within the responder subgroup is partly due to feature_023 being a population-prevalence enrichment marker, not a true effect modifier - it does not interact with feature_013 once feature_029 and feature_028 are conditioned on.

analyses.code: sub_pos = df[(df.feature_029==1) & (df.feature_028==0) & (df.feature_018==0) & (df.feature_033<=15)]; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_023', sub_pos).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within full biomarker+ subgroup, conditional TE: feature_023=0 TE=+4.33 (n0=1118, n1=593) vs feature_023=1 TE=+4.71 (n0=6359, n1=3505). Difference of 0.38 months; small relative to >4 month effect. feature_023 contributes mildly but is not required for the responder definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When the marginal feature_013 effect is partitioned across the four cells defined by (biomarker_pos, feature_033<=15), >99% of the absolute treatment effect is concentrated in the cell {biomarker_pos=1, feature_033<=15}.

analyses.result_summary: TE by 4 cells of (3-binary biomarker_pos, f033<=15): (0,0) TE=-0.003 (n=11980); (0,1) TE=+0.007 (n=19339); (1,0) TE=+0.021 (n=7106); (1,1) TE=+4.651 (n=11575). Treatment effect is essentially zero in three cells and +4.65 in the fourth. >99% of population-attributable treatment benefit comes from the 23% of patients in cell (1,1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_013 x continuous interactions to the multivariable PFS model improves fit only via feature_013:feature_033; including feature_013:feature_012 or feature_013:feature_024 does not materially improve R^2.

analyses.result_summary: OLS with feature_013*biomarker_pos achieves R^2=0.792 (matches full model with 37 features). Adding feature_013:feature_012 or feature_013:feature_024 gives p~0.04 and p~0.41 respectively, with negligible R^2 gain. The 4-feature predictive biomarker captures essentially all systematic treatment heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-positive subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 & feature_033<=15), feature_013 = 1 increases mean pfs_months from approximately 4.29 to approximately 8.94 (absolute difference ~+4.65 months).

analyses.result_summary: biomarker_pos=1 (full 4-marker), feature_013=0 -> mean=4.286, sd=2.130, n=7477; feature_013=1 -> mean=8.936, sd=2.493, n=4098. Absolute difference +4.650 months; t-test p < 1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When feature_013 is regressed on pfs_months in the full cohort with feature_013*biomarker_pos as the only systematic interaction, the main-effect feature_013 coefficient becomes statistically null (p > 0.5) and biomarker_pos main effect on PFS in untreated patients is approximately 0 - i.e., the biomarker is purely predictive, not prognostic.

analyses.result_summary: Coefficients: intercept 4.317; feature_013 +0.006 (p=0.79); biomarker_pos -0.032 (p=0.26); feature_013:biomarker_pos +4.644 (p<1e-300). Without treatment, biomarker_pos patients have PFS indistinguishable from biomarker-negative; with treatment, they gain ~4.65 months while biomarker-negative patients gain nothing. This is a classic predictive (not prognostic) biomarker pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_023=1 to the responder definition increases the average treatment effect to ~+4.7 months but at the cost of excluding ~15% of true responders (n drops from 11575 to ~9864). The 4-marker definition (without feature_023) is the parsimonious optimum.

analyses.result_summary: 4-marker (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 & feature_033<=15): n=11575, TE=+4.651. 5-marker adding feature_023=1: n=9864 of those, TE=+4.71. Marginal gain ~0.06 months with ~15% smaller subgroup. Not worth adding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no clinically meaningful subgroup outside the 4-marker biomarker+ definition in which feature_013 confers a positive treatment effect (i.e., the responder definition is exhaustive).

analyses.code: Inspect TE within each binary-stratified cell outside the biomarker+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Outside the 4-marker biomarker+ subgroup (n=38425), no binary stratification produces a treatment effect with absolute value > 0.2 months. Treatment effect is essentially zero everywhere outside the responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP: Patients receiving the treatment indicated by feature_013=1 will experience a mean pfs_months that is approximately +4.65 months (95% CI ~+4.55 to +4.74) longer than identically biomarker-defined patients receiving feature_013=0 IF AND ONLY IF they meet ALL FOUR of the predicates: feature_029 = 1, feature_028 = 0, feature_018 = 0, feature_033 <= 15. Outside this 4-predicate subgroup, the treatment effect of feature_013 on pfs_months is statistically indistinguishable from zero (point estimate +0.006 months, 95% CI roughly -0.04 to +0.05).

FINAL PROGNOSTIC FACTORS for pfs_months independent of treatment (in OLS with 37 features): feature_012 (positive, +0.176/unit), feature_005 (negative, -1.17/level), feature_006 (negative, -1.55), feature_001 (negative, -0.98), feature_029 (positive, +0.53), feature_028 (negative, -0.58), feature_018 (negative, -0.47), feature_024 (positive, +0.48/unit), feature_033 (negative, -0.019/unit, but acts mainly through treatment-effect modification within biomarker+ subgroup), feature_004 (negative, -0.077/unit). Multivariable R^2 = 0.792.

analyses.result_summary: Final confirmation: interaction coefficient feature_013:biomarker_pos = +4.644 months (95% CI 4.551-4.738, p<1e-300). Within biomarker+ (n=11575): mean PFS untreated 4.286, treated 8.936, absolute gain +4.65 months. Outside (n=38425): treated 4.324 vs untreated 4.317, gain +0.006 months (p=0.78). The final responder predicate is necessary in all four components and sufficient.

Final OLS R^2=0.792 across n=50000. Coefficients (sign and magnitude): feature_012 +0.176/unit (range 30-90 -> ~10 month spread), feature_005 -1.172/level (range 0-2 -> 2.34 mo), feature_006 -1.549, feature_001 -0.983, feature_029 +0.532, feature_028 -0.582, feature_018 -0.471, feature_024 +0.476/unit, feature_033 -0.019/unit, feature_004 -0.077/unit, feature_013 +1.101 (marginal, but conditional on biomarker effectively zero outside biomarker+ subgroup).
